perturbation.original_text: Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement without further legal liability in the case that Party B does not comply with Party A's management and arrangement.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement without further legal liability in the case that Party B does not comply with Party A's management and arrangement. However, Party B reserves the right to seek compensation for loss of income due to early termination if Party A exercises this right within the first six months of any given year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Party A the right to terminate the agreement without legal liability if Party B doesn't comply with their management. The added text, however, allows Party B to seek compensation if Party A terminates within the first six months of the year. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding Party A's liability upon termination, thus creating uncertainty about termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) During the transportation, Part B shall completely comply with Party A's transportation arrangement and relevant systems. Party B shall bear any consequences and legal liability arising out of Party B's non-compliance, and Party A shall have no liability. Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement without further legal liability in the case that Party B does not comply with Party A's management and arrangement. However, Party B reserves the right to seek compensation for loss of income due to early termination if Party A exercises this right within the first six months of any given year.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text state that Party A has no liability if they terminate the agreement due to Party B's non-compliance. The changed text adds a clause stating that Party B can seek compensation if Party A terminates within the first six months of the year. This directly contradicts the 'no liability' clause, creating an inconsistency regarding Party A's financial responsibility upon termination, in alignment with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement. Party B shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement, unless Party B experiences unforeseen economic hardship. Party B shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent, except in situations of documented force majeure, in which case no liquidated damages will be required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Party B needs Party A's written consent for early termination and must pay liquidated damages if they terminate without consent. The inserted text allows Party B to terminate without consent if they experience 'unforeseen economic hardship', and waives liquidated damages in the event of 'force majeure'. This contradicts the original requirement for written consent and automatic liquidated damages, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the conditions for early termination and associated penalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B shall obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement, unless Party B experiences unforeseen economic hardship. Party B shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent, except in situations of documented force majeure, in which case no liquidated damages will be required. Within the contract period, Party B shall not charge the freight difference if Party A rents same-level vehicles. Party B shall also compensate Party A's other losses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces exceptions ('unforeseen economic hardship' and 'force majeure') to the original requirement for Party A's written consent for early termination and the associated liquidated damages. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The introduction of these exceptions directly conflicts with the initial statement that Party B 'shall obtain Party A's written consent' and 'shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages', creating a clear contradiction regarding the conditions under which early termination can occur without consent or penalty. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting obligations. The changed text effectively weakens the initial obligation, creating an in-text contradiction.
